players.name: Mohamed El Shenawy
players.full_name: Mohamed El-Sayed Mohamed El-Shenawy Gomaa; محمد الشناوي
players.bio: Egypt's long-serving shot-stopper has been the Pharaohs' last line of defence for over a decade. The Al Ahly stalwart impressed at the 2018 World Cup despite Egypt's early exit. At 37, this is likely his World Cup swansong.
players.facts: Was Egypt's hero at the 2017 AFCON final, helping them reach their first final in 31 years.

players.name: Yasser Ibrahim
players.full_name: Yasser Ibrahim Ahmed El Hanafi; يَاسِر إبرَاهِيْم أَحمَد الْحَنَفِي
players.bio: A composed and powerful centre-back who has been a reliable figure in Al Ahly's trophy-laden defence. With 17 caps and one international goal, Ibrahim brings experience from Africa's most decorated club to his national duties.
players.facts: Has won more CAF Champions League medals than most players win domestic titles in a career.

players.name: Mohamed Hany
players.full_name: Mohamed Hany Gamal El-Demerdash; محمد هاني الدمرداش
players.bio: An energetic right-back with an eye for overlapping runs, Mohamed Hany has established himself as Egypt's first-choice option on the right flank. His 42 caps reflect consistent trust from the national setup over several years.
players.facts: Born in Cairo on Groundhog Day — 2 February — meaning he always knows what's coming next.

players.name: Hossam Abdelmaguid
players.full_name: Hossam Abdelmaguid Abdelsalam, حسام عبد المجيد عبد السلام;
players.bio: One of the youngest defenders in the squad, the Zamalek centre-back arrives at his first World Cup aged just 25. A commanding presence for Egypt's great Cairo rivals, Abdelmaguid has the world at his feet.
players.facts: Born in 2001, he could face opponents older than his international career is long — 13 caps and counting.

players.name: Ramy Rabia
players.full_name: Ramy Hisham Abdel Aziz Mostafa Rabia; رامى ربيعة
players.bio: A composed ball-playing centre-back with a surprisingly prolific record — five international goals is impressive for a defender. Now plying his trade in the UAE with Al Ain, Rabia brings veteran nous to Egypt's backline.
players.facts: With 5 international goals, Rabia has outscored several of Egypt's recognised forwards — remarkable for a centre-back.

players.name: Mohamed Abdelmonem
players.full_name: Mohamed Abdelmonem El-Sayed Mohamed Ahmed; محمد عبد المنعم
players.bio: The Nice defender is one of Egypt's most exciting exports to European football. Commanding in the air and assured on the ball, Abdelmonem has 36 caps at just 27 and brings Ligue 1 pedigree to the Pharaohs' defence.
players.facts: Made the move to French football with Nice, becoming one of the few Egyptian defenders to thrive in a top-5 European league.

players.name: Trézéguet
players.full_name: Mahmoud Ahmed Ibrahim Hassan; محمود حسن
players.bio: Known simply as Trezeguet — inspired by French legend David Trezeguet — the winger made his name at Kasımpaşa and Aston Villa before returning to Al Ahly. Nearly 100 caps and 23 goals make him a national icon.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Vapor XIII Elite FG
players.facts: Named after French World Cup winner David Trezeguet by his football-mad father. No pressure there, then.

players.name: Emam Ashour
players.full_name: Emam Ashour Metwally Abdelghany; إمام عاشور
players.bio: A dynamic central midfielder with energy to burn, Emam Ashour has become a key cog in Al Ahly's engine room. His 29 caps reflect growing importance in the national setup as Egypt look to control midfield battles.
players.facts: Hails from El Senbellawein — a city whose name is arguably harder to pronounce than his first touch is to dispossess.

players.name: Hamza Abdelkarim
players.full_name: Hamza Mohamed Abdelkarim Selim, حمزة عبدالكريم;
players.bio: Barely 18 years old and already training with Barcelona B, Hamza Abdelkarim is the wildcard of the squad. With just 2 caps, he arrives in North America as Egypt's most exciting prospect — and possibly the tournament's youngest player.
players.facts: Born in 2008 — the year Egypt won their sixth Africa Cup of Nations. A good omen, perhaps.

players.name: Mohamed Salah
players.full_name: Mohamed Salah Hamed Mahrous Ghaly; محمد صلاح حامد محروس غالى‎
players.bio: The Egyptian King needs little introduction. Liverpool's all-time top scorer and serial Premier League golden boot winner, Salah is arguably the greatest player in Egyptian history. At 33, he arrives hungry to make his World Cup mark.
players.facts: Salah has scored more Premier League goals than any Egyptian — or African — player in history. A whole country on his shoulders.

players.name: Mostafa Ziko
players.full_name: Mostafa Mohamed Zaki Abdelraouf; مصطفي زيكو
players.bio: A deadly finisher for Pyramids FC, Mostafa Ziko has a remarkable international strike rate — two goals in just two caps. The Alexandrian winger arrives at the World Cup with plenty to prove on the grandest stage.
players.facts: Two caps, two goals. A 100% scoring rate that even Salah can't match on debut form. Statistical perfection.

players.name: Haissem Hassan
players.full_name: هيثم حسن
players.bio: Born in Bagnolet, France, Haissem Hassan chose Egypt over Les Bleus and is carving out a career at Oviedo in Spain's second tier. The young winger brings European flair and dual-culture perspective to the Pharaohs' attack.
players.facts: Born in France, plays in Spain, represents Egypt. Haissem Hassan is international football's true citizen of the world.

players.name: Ahmed Fatouh
players.full_name: Ahmed Mohamed Abou El Fotouh Mohamed, أحمد محمد أبو الفتوح محمد;
players.bio: A reliable left-back with nearly 40 caps, Ahmed Fatouh has been a consistent servant of the Pharaohs since emerging through Zamalek's ranks. Solid defensively and capable going forward, he's a dependable presence on the left flank.
players.facts: Has represented Zamalek throughout his career — one of Egyptian football's truest one-club men at international level.

players.name: Hamdy Fathy
players.full_name: Hamdy Fathy Abdelhalim Abdelfattah; حمدي فتحى
players.bio: A tenacious defensive midfielder with 63 caps, Hamdy Fathy has long been the man tasked with winning the ball back so others can shine. Currently in Qatar with Al-Wakrah, he brings discipline and experience to Egypt's midfield.
players.facts: Has spent time in Qatar's league, meaning he's played in two of the last three World Cup host nations. Well-travelled indeed.

players.name: Karim Hafez
players.full_name: Karim Hafez Ramadan Seifeldin; كَرِيم حَافِظ رَمَضَان سَيْف الدِّين
players.bio: A Beheira-born left-back who has worked his way into the national picture through consistent performances for Pyramids FC. With nine caps at 30, Hafez knows this World Cup could define his international legacy.
players.facts: Shares a birthplace region with teammate Hamdy Fathy — Beheira clearly produces quality full-backs.

players.name: El Mahdy Soliman
players.full_name: El Mahdy Soliman, المهدي سليمان;
players.bio: The elder statesman of Egypt's goalkeeping corps at 39, El Mahdy Soliman brings a lifetime of experience from Zamalek. Uncapped at international level, he serves as the squad's wise old glove man and dressing room presence.
players.facts: At 39 with zero caps, Soliman is proof that hope springs eternal — the oldest uncapped keeper at the 2026 World Cup.

players.name: Mohanad Lasheen
players.full_name: Mohanad Mostafa Ahmed Abdelmonem Lasheen; مهند مصطفى لاشين
players.bio: A hard-working defensive midfielder, Mohanad Lasheen has made 23 appearances for Egypt after coming through the Pyramids system. Often the unsung hero shielding the defence, he does the dirty work so flair players can flourish.
players.facts: His role is simple: win the ball, give it to Salah. He's very good at that first part.

players.name: Nabil Emad
players.full_name: Nabil Emad Aly Elmahdy; نبيل عماد علي المهدي
players.bio: A disciplined defensive midfielder from El Mahalla El Kubra, Nabil Emad has earned 12 caps despite playing in Bahrain's league with Al-Najma. His selection underlines Egypt's depth in the holding midfield position.
players.facts: Plays club football in Bahrain — making him one of the very few World Cup players based in the Gulf's smaller leagues.

players.name: Marwan Attia
players.full_name: Marwan Attia Fahim Ghallab; مروان عطية
players.bio: An industrious defensive midfielder at Al Ahly, Marwan Attia has accumulated 34 caps with a knack for breaking up opposition play. Hailing from Kafr El Dawwar, he's a quietly essential piece of Egypt's midfield puzzle.
players.facts: Has played alongside some of Africa's greatest club players at Al Ahly — the perfect finishing school for any midfielder.

players.name: Ibrahim Adel
players.full_name: Ibrahim Adel Ali Mohamed Hassan, إِبْرَاهِيم عَادِل عَلِيّ مُحَمَّد حَسَن;
players.bio: Port Said's most exciting export, Ibrahim Adel plies his trade in Denmark with Nordsjælland — a club famous for producing global talent. Three international goals in 24 caps show a winger with ambition and end product to match.
players.facts: Nordsjælland is famed for its global talent pipeline. Ibrahim Adel is proof Egypt fits perfectly into that picture.

players.name: Mahmoud Saber
players.full_name: Mahmoud Saber Abdelmohsen Hassan, مَحْمُود صَابِر عَبْد الْمُحْسِن حَسَن‎;
players.bio: A 24-year-old central midfielder with ZED FC, Mahmoud Saber has quickly earned 15 caps and chipped in with an international goal. One of the younger domestic-based midfielders in the squad, he's hungry to impress on the world stage.
players.facts: Plays for ZED FC — named after an Egyptian real estate company. Even club football has a corporate sponsor these days.

players.name: Omar Marmoush
players.full_name: Omar Khaled Mohamed Abd Elsala Marmoush; مَر خَالِد مُحَمَّد مَرمُوش
players.bio: The Manchester City forward took the Bundesliga by storm at Eintracht Frankfurt before Pep Guardiola came calling. Versatile, lightning-quick and a proven scorer, Marmoush gives Egypt a potent second star alongside Salah.
players.facts: Scored 24 Bundesliga goals in 2024/25 to earn his City move — proving Egyptian forwards can light up Europe's biggest stages.

players.name: Mostafa Shobeir
players.full_name: Mostafa Ahmed Abdelaziz Mohamed Shobeir; مصطفي أحمد شوبير
players.bio: Al Ahly's young goalkeeper has nine caps and a stellar future ahead of him. Born in Giza in 2000, Shobeir represents the next generation between the sticks for the Pharaohs, learning from veterans at club and international level.
players.facts: Named Shobeir — Arabic for 'one who is patient.' A very useful quality for a goalkeeper waiting for his moment to shine.

players.name: Tarek Alaa
players.full_name: Tarek Alaa El Gebaly, طارق علاء الجبالي
players.bio: A young right-back with just three caps to his name, Tarek Alaa arrives at the World Cup as one of Egypt's fresher faces. The ZED FC defender has youth on his side at 24 and plenty of time to make this moment count.
players.facts: Born on 5 January 2002 — making him one of a rare batch of players who can say they're a World Cup star at just 24.

players.name: Zizo
players.full_name: Ahmed Mostafa Mohamed Sayed; أحمد سيد زيزو
players.bio: Known simply as Zizo — never a bad sign — this Al Ahly winger has racked up 63 caps and five international goals since emerging as one of Egypt's most creative right-sided threats. Technically gifted and a firm fan favourite.
players.facts: One of Egypt's most recognisable one-name footballers after Salah and Trezeguet. Cairo clearly has a thing for nicknames.

players.name: Mohamed Alaa
players.full_name: محمد علاء
players.bio: El Gouna's shot-stopper arrives at the World Cup uncapped but deserving his place as depth cover. The 27-year-old has earned his call-up through consistent performances in the Egyptian Premier League and will be ready if called upon.
players.facts: El Gouna FC is based in a Red Sea resort town — making Mohamed Alaa arguably the most scenic-commuted keeper in world football.
